title: Knysna declared local disaster amid water crisis
bodyText:
[{"type":"paragraph","text":"KNYSNA NEWS - Although greater Knysna experienced some relief following rainfall yesterday and the sight of water flowing over the Gouna River weir, the amount received is unfortunately insufficient to remove the municipality from disaster conditions.","html":"KNYSNA NEWS - Although greater Knysna experienced some relief following rainfall yesterday and the sight of water flowing over the Gouna River weir, the amount received is unfortunately insufficient to remove the municipality from disaster conditions."},{"type":"blockquote","text":"As a result, the Knysna Council has declared the greater Knysna municipal area a local disaster area in terms of the Disaster Management Act, Act 57 of 2002."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Council decision enables emergency interventionKnysna Executive Mayor, Thando Matika, confirmed that the declaration was approved during a special Council meeting held today, Friday 16 January.","html":"Council decision enables emergency intervention
Knysna Executive Mayor, Thando Matika, confirmed that the declaration was approved during a special Council meeting held today, Friday 16 January."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"\"This decision allows for urgent provincial and national interventions,\" said Matika. \"It also empowers the Mayor to authorise unforeseen and unavoidable expenditure in emergency circumstances where no provision exists in the approved budget.\"","html":"\"This decision allows for urgent provincial and national interventions,\" said Matika. \"It also empowers the Mayor to authorise unforeseen and unavoidable expenditure in emergency circumstances where no provision exists in the approved budget.\""},{"type":"heading","text":"Disaster management structures activated","level":"h3"},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Matika said the water emergency will be managed in accordance with the Knysna Municipality Disaster Management Plan.","html":"Matika said the water emergency will be managed in accordance with the Knysna Municipality Disaster Management Plan."},{"type":"blockquote","text":"The Garden Route District Disaster Management Centre, in consultation with the Knysna Local Disaster Management Unit, will coordinate short- and medium-term interventions required to address the crisis."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"The Western Cape Department of Agriculture will also be requested to conduct an urgent assessment of the drought’s impact on agricultural areas within the municipal boundaries.","html":"The Western Cape Department of Agriculture will also be requested to conduct an urgent assessment of the drought’s impact on agricultural areas within the municipal boundaries."},{"type":"heading","text":"Water augmentation programme accelerated","level":"h3"},{"type":"paragraph","text":"\"Specialist teams from provincial government and the private sector have completed geohydrological assessments and engineering studies to ensure that all interventions are effective, sustainable and environmentally responsible,\" Matika said.","html":"\"Specialist teams from provincial government and the private sector have completed geohydrological assessments and engineering studies to ensure that all interventions are effective, sustainable and environmentally responsible,\" Matika said."},{"type":"heading","text":"Augmentation projects nearing completion","level":"h3"},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Several water augmentation projects are already underway and are expected to be operational within the next two weeks.","html":"Several water augmentation projects are already underway and are expected to be operational within the next two weeks."},{"type":"blockquote","text":"These include the development of the Bigai and Bongani springs, as well as the refurbishment of seven existing boreholes, collectively expected to deliver approximately 3,2 megalitres of water per day."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"A further seven new boreholes will be drilled, with an anticipated yield of an additional 2,7 megalitres per day. Additional augmentation options are currently being investigated.","html":"A further seven new boreholes will be drilled, with an anticipated yield of an additional 2,7 megalitres per day. Additional augmentation options are currently being investigated."},{"type":"heading","text":"Private sector support strengthens supply","level":"h3"},{"type":"paragraph","text":"\"When all current augmentation initiatives are fully implemented, the total expected additional potable water supply will be in the region of 11 megalitres per day,\" he said.","html":"\"When all current augmentation initiatives are fully implemented, the total expected additional potable water supply will be in the region of 11 megalitres per day,\" he said."},{"type":"heading","text":"Funding applications submitted","level":"h3"},{"type":"paragraph","text":"A funding application was submitted to the Department of Water and Sanitation on 14 January, and final feedback is currently pending.","html":"A funding application was submitted to the Department of Water and Sanitation on 14 January, and final feedback is currently pending."},{"type":"heading","text":"Day zero drawing closer","level":"h3"},{"type":"paragraph","text":"\"These interventions are critical for securing water supply in the short to medium term,\" Matika said. \"However, we must acknowledge that Day Zero is approaching.\"","html":"\"These interventions are critical for securing water supply in the short to medium term,\" Matika said. \"However, we must acknowledge that Day Zero is approaching.\""},{"type":"blockquote","text":"He emphasised that the only way to postpone or avoid Day Zero is through immediate and drastic reductions in water consumption."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"\"Day Zero does not mean Knysna will run out of water entirely,\" he explained. \"It means the reticulation system will be shut down to prevent total system collapse, and residents will have to queue daily for personal water rations.\"","html":"\"Day Zero does not mean Knysna will run out of water entirely,\" he explained. \"It means the reticulation system will be shut down to prevent total system collapse, and residents will have to queue daily for personal water rations.\""},{"type":"heading","text":"Dam levels at a critical stage","level":"h3"},{"type":"blockquote","text":"Water supply is supplemented through continued abstraction from the Glebe Dam, which currently stands at around 60% capacity, equating to approximately six days’ supply, as well as contributions from the Bigai spring and existing boreholes."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"While around 50mm of rainfall was recorded, this has already been factored into water supply calculations and remains insufficient to resolve the crisis.","html":"While around 50mm of rainfall was recorded, this has already been factored into water supply calculations and remains insufficient to resolve the crisis."},{"type":"heading","text":"Demand reduction and infrastructure response","level":"h3"},{"type":"blockquote","text":"Level 4 water restrictions will be communicated, monitored and intensified, with flow restrictors installed where non-compliance is identified."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"To address water losses caused by pipe bursts and aging infrastructure, the municipality has appointed additional temporary plumbing teams, including five plumbers, six water meter installers and 30 general assistants. This is expected to reduce response times to major pipe bursts to between four and six hours.","html":"To address water losses caused by pipe bursts and aging infrastructure, the municipality has appointed additional temporary plumbing teams, including five plumbers, six water meter installers and 30 general assistants. This is expected to reduce response times to major pipe bursts to between four and six hours."},{"type":"heading","text":"Call for public cooperation","level":"h3"},{"type":"paragraph","text":"The choices we make now will determine whether we avoid Day Zero or face daily water rationing. Collective action is essential.","html":"The choices we make now will determine whether we avoid Day Zero or face daily water rationing. Collective action is essential."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Read previous articles:","html":"Read previous articles:"},{"type":"list","items":["Rain brings brief relief for Knysna","Knysna races to avert day zero","No water cut-offs in Knysna yet as crisis unfolds","Joint site visits Strengthen Knysna's water supply"],"ordered":false},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Knysna River Pumping Station area prior to rainfall. This picture was taken on 10 January. Photo: Supplied","html":"Knysna River Pumping Station area prior to rainfall. This picture was taken on 10 January. Photo: Supplied"},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Knysna River Pumping Station yesterday evening Thursday 15 January at 19:40. Photo: Supplied","html":"Knysna River Pumping Station yesterday evening Thursday 15 January at 19:40. Photo: Supplied"},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Knysna River Pumping Station yesterday 15 January at 17:00. Photo: Supplied","html":"Knysna River Pumping Station yesterday 15 January at 17:00. Photo: Supplied"},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Water making its way downstream from Gouna River enroute Charlesford. Photo: Supplied","html":"Water making its way downstream from Gouna River enroute Charlesford. Photo: Supplied"},{"type":"paragraph","text":"‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’","html":"‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’"}]
